turns.label: tax question
turns.message: Quick question — do I charge GST on labour and materials separately or combined?
turns.reply:
GST is generally charged on the taxable supply as a whole, so you usually apply GST to each taxable line item rather than “combined” as a single special category. If labour and materials are both taxable, both attract GST.

If you’re registered for GST, the invoice can show GST on each line or as a total GST amount at the end — the result is the same. If one item is GST-free or input taxed, that line is treated differently.

📎 Source: ato.gov.au  
⚠️ Booky is not an accountant or financial adviser. This is general info from public sources — please check with a qualified professional for your specific situation.

turns.label: gst question
turns.message: Am I supposed to register for GST as a rideshare driver?
turns.reply:
Yes — if you’re a rideshare driver in Australia, you generally need to register for GST regardless of turnover.

Rideshare is treated differently from most small businesses, so GST registration is required even if you’re under the usual $75,000 threshold. You’ll also need to charge GST on fares and report it through BAS.

📎 Source: ato.gov.au  
⚠️ Booky is not an accountant or financial adviser. This is general info from public sources — please check with a qualified professional for your specific situation.

turns.label: follow-up question
turns.message: How do I claim car expenses?
turns.reply:
You can usually claim car expenses using one of two methods:

• Cents per kilometre: claim a set rate per business km, up to the ATO limit.
• Logbook method: claim the business-use percentage of actual running costs, based on a valid logbook.

Common claimable costs can include fuel, servicing, rego, insurance, tyres, repairs, and depreciation, but the method you use affects what you can claim.

📎 Source: ato.gov.au  
⚠️ Booky is not an accountant or financial adviser. This is general info from public sources — please check with a qualified professional for your specific situation.
